author: Steve Griffing PE(CSE) CISSP-ISSEP CPP
rev_l_delta_note: Rev L adds no new hardware vs Rev K dual-EDF. The PID closed-loop governor runs on the existing AM6232 M4F coprocessor (firmware update only). Budget and high-performance EDF options are documented in edf_options section. Hardware BOM is identical to bom_revK.json.
hardware_reference: bom_revK.json — all hull, propulsion, avionics, wiring, payload, sensor, and radio items are identical to Rev K dual-EDF. See bom_revK.json for full part-level detail.

rev_l_firmware.desc: Rev L PID governor firmware binary — M4F coprocessor partition
rev_l_firmware.target: AM6232 M4F coprocessor on all 4 FC nodes (Cape-A: FC1–FC4)
rev_l_firmware.flash_method: CN1 GCS upload → Ethernet ring → TPM 2.0 verified flash on each FC node
rev_l_firmware.fault_latch_policy: Latched per-ESC for flight. Ground power cycle + GCS ack required to clear.
rev_l_firmware.fault_both_action: NACELLE_LOST flag to FC → ArduPilot RTH mandatory

calibration.output: EDF_THRUST_K (g/RPM²) — fitted from bench thrust vs RPM sweep
calibration.notes: Re-run after any EDF replacement. k varies ±5% unit-to-unit.

mavlink_reporting.trigger: state transition to DERATE or FAULT
per-EDF health bit in custom extension

edf_options.note: Budget EDFs (Phases 2–6) prove the airframe cheaply. XRP 3660-2700KV is the Phase 7 baseline. High-perf options available for Phase 7+ if T/W >3.5 desired. The PID governor auto-tunes to any option via bench calibration — only EDF_THRUST_K changes.
edf_options.governor_compat: Rev L governor compatible with any 80mm 6S EDF. Re-run governor_cal.py after EDF swap.
edf_options.fuselage_note: XFLY Galaxy X4 PRO 40mm 12-blade 4S 5850KV is the ONLY approved fuselage EDF. Do NOT substitute.

tiers.purpose: Prove airframe, avionics, and controls cheaply before expensive motor investment

options.name: FlyFox 80mm 6S 2900KV 12-blade
options.nacelle_pair_note: 2× FlyFox tandem × 0.91 efficiency = 2548g/nacelle. Aircraft total 5746g. T/W 1.59 (AUW 3607g). Below 2.0 — acceptable for airframe test, NOT for payload ops.

options.name: Freewing 80mm 2836-2150KV 6S
options.nacelle_pair_note: 2× Freewing tandem × 0.91 = 3094g/nacelle. Aircraft total 6838g. T/W 1.90 — close to 2.0, light cargo possible.
options.phase_use: Phases 2–6, acceptable for light payload test

options.name: Changesun XRP 3660-2700KV 80mm 6S
options.nacelle_pair_note: 2× XRP tandem × 0.91 = 5278g/nacelle. Aircraft 11206g ≈ 11250g spec (rounding). T/W 3.11:1 empty.
options.notes: Best thrust/dollar in class for 80mm 6S. Highest available KV at this frame size. 84A draw requires 120A ESC — never use 50A or 80A ESC with XRP.

tiers.purpose: T/W > 3.5 target, maximum payload, aircraft-grade hardware

options.name: Freewing 80mm 12-blade 3500KV 6S
options.notes: Drop-in upgrade from XRP. Same 120A ESC already installed.

options.name: Jetfan 80mm Pro 3300KV 6S
options.notes: Precision-balanced CNC aluminum housing. Excellent efficiency.

options.name: Schübeler DS-51-AXI HST 80mm 6S
options.notes: German aircraft-grade EDF. Maximum thrust for 80mm 6S class. T/W >4.0 enables aggressive aerobatic envelopes. Cost: $640/pair for 2 nacelles.

upgrade_procedure: Power off. Disconnect all ESC motor leads.
Remove nacelle pod (4× M3 screws from tilt bracket). EDF slides out of pod.
Install new EDF. Reconnect motor leads (check rotation — reverse 2 of 3 if wrong).
Run governor bench calibration: python3 governor_cal.py --mode bench
Update EDF_THRUST_K in governor_config.h from bench output.
Flash updated governor_config.h binary to FC1–FC4.
Verify BDSHOT RPM on all 4 ESCs at idle. Confirm equalization within ±100 RPM.

performance.nacelle_thrust_note: Two 80mm 6S EDFs in tandem series per nacelle. Combined ≈91% of arithmetic 2×2900g = 5300g/nacelle. Flow-straightener vanes in 20mm inter-EDF gap recover ~40% of tangential velocity loss.
performance.rev_l_notes: Rev L is a firmware-only update from Rev K dual-EDF. Hardware BOM unchanged. The PID governor (M4F 500Hz) provides: per-EDF RPM tracking PID, cooperative nacelle equalization (FWD/AFT RPM matched ±2% bias), thermal derate 85–110°C, current limiting 80A soft/105A hard, fault latch per ESC, MAVLink fault reporting, and RTH on nacelle loss. EDF options (budget, standard XRP, high-perf Schübeler) documented in edf_options section — governor auto-tunes via bench calibration.

total_auw_g.note: Rev L — 2× 80mm 6S series EDFs per nacelle + PID governor (firmware only) + 4× 120A ESCs + 8× PocketBeagle 2
total_auw_g.note_power: Each nacelle draws 168A peak (2× 84A ESCs). Main bus: 4-AWG silicone, XT90-S per nacelle rail. PDB handles 350A peak (2× 168A + 30A fuselage). 300A copper bus bar required.
total_auw_g.note_governor: Rev L PID governor adds zero mass. M4F execution is on existing AM6232 silicon. governor_firmware_revL.bin is distributed via Ethernet ring from CN1 GCS upload.

build_notes.rev_l_vs_rev_k: Rev L requires no hardware changes from Rev K dual-EDF build. If building from scratch, follow bom_revK.json phase structure. Rev L only adds governor firmware commissioning (governor_cal.py bench run + PID step-response tuning) after Phase 7 hardware installation.
build_notes.governor_commissioning_phase: Phase 7 — after EDF installation, before first full-power flight
governor_commissioning_steps: Enable BDSHOT on all 4 nacelle ESCs in BLHeli32/AM32 Configurator
Set serial telem baud 115200 on all 4 nacelle ESCs
Flash governor_firmware_revL.bin to FC1–FC4 via CN1 GCS upload
Run: python3 governor_cal.py --mode bench (per-nacelle thrust stand sweep)
Update EDF_THRUST_K from bench output in governor_config.h
Flash updated config binary to FC1–FC4
Fault injection test: FAULT_INJECT_OVERTEMP per ESC, verify latch behavior
Flight acceptance: two hover cycles, monitor temp/current/RPM via GCS
build_notes.edf_swap_commissioning: After any EDF replacement: re-run governor_cal.py bench sweep. Update EDF_THRUST_K. No PID re-tuning required for same-class EDFs.
build_notes.faa_registration: Replace N00000 PLACEHOLDER on decal sheet before first untethered flight.
